question.text: In commodity futures, only a fraction of contracts leads to actual physical delivery. This is because:

answer.text: Most participants square off positions before expiry or use cash settlement
Only hedgers are allowed to take delivery

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Only a fraction of futures contracts lead to physical delivery because most speculators and traders square off (close) positions before expiry. Forward contracts, by contrast, generally result in physical delivery. **The Trap:** Exchanges allow physical delivery. Storage is available through accredited warehouses. Both hedgers and speculators can take/give delivery if they maintain positions. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII equity derivatives are almost entirely cash-settled — a contrast to commodity futures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clients who don't intend to take/give physical delivery should square off positions well before the delivery period — late exits may face reduced liquidity and wider spreads.
